title: The looming El Niño could be bad – but much worse is to come
description: Global warming will amplify the impacts of El Niño events, and could also make them much stronger and more far-reaching
author: Michael Le Page

title: Earth has a mysterious triple symmetry that may influence its climate
description: A circle running along the 27° east and 153° west meridians divides the globe into two halves with equal reflectivity – and this may have implications for solar geoengineering schemes
author: James Woodford

title: Ditch the niceties in AI prompts to save energy use, say researchers
description: A UN report warns of the rapid growth in AI energy consumption, but suggests users can improve efficiency by making prompts more concise
author: Luke Taylor

title: Atom-based quantum computers are catching up in the race to usefulness
description: A quantum computer made from extremely cold atoms can correct its own errors during long computations, an important prerequisite for becoming truly useful
author: Karmela Padavic-Callaghan

title: Keto diet shows real promise for anorexia recovery
description: Restricting carbohydrates may sound like an unlikely approach to treating anorexia, but following a ketogenic diet was linked to recovery in 3 in 4 people with the eating disorder in a small trial
author: Caroline Williams

title: Ötzi's frozen remains may harbour metabolically active microbes
description: Researchers studying a 5300-year-old mummified man have identified bacteria that lived in his gut when he was alive, as well as cold-tolerant fungi that colonised his body after death
author: Chris Simms
